title: quercus robur f. fastigiata
intro: Quercus robur f. fastigiata is a cultivar of the English oak tree (Quercus robur), known for its narrow, columnar growth habit, making it a popular choice for landscaping and urban spaces. The bark and leaves of Quercus robur f. fastigiata contain a range of medicinal compounds, including flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is traditionally used to support cardiovascular health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its ability to relax the airways and reduce inflammation. Quercus robur f. fastigiata is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and can also be used as a component in traditional herbal remedies, such as infusions and salves, to promote overall well-being and address various health concerns.
